inline_comments.message: I think it will not work when we have multiple containers.
To be removed if retry is not implemented.
To be removed if retry is not implemented.

To be removed if retry is not implemented.
To be removed if retry is not implemented.

usually Enable Annotations are defined in a configuration class. Is there any benefit of defining it here? Does it restrict the code which it considers for retry to this package or it is still global?
providing it in filter will make it easier to read.
As per existing functionality, can we store two yang resources when creating schema-set?
To be removed if retry is not implemented.
To be removed if retry is not implemented.c
To be removed if retry is not implemented.
The changed is made for the configuration as it is confirmed that it does not restrict the scope of the retry to have it here.

Probably it could be. As I think that the team has a preference for typed variables and as long my local Sonar is not complaining, I'm not using var. Could it be possible that my local Sonar is not in sync with ONAP one ? Anyway, if it finally appears that this is an issue for ONAP Sonar, we'll make the change.

I'm not sure about the filtering you are thinking about. Is it something like:

    List<YangResourceEntity> filteredYangResourceEntities =
            yangResourceEntities
                .stream()
                .filter(entity ->
                        constraintException.getSQLException().getMessage()
                                .contains(entity.getChecksum()))
                .collect(Collectors.toList());

Anything else ?

Exact, several yang resources can be associated to the schema set to be created.

And one of these yang resource is causing the DataIntegrityViolationException. Here we just want to have this specific yang resource that caused the sql error in the business exception in order to have the information in the logs.

I find lambda a little difficult to understand, if it has more than couple of lines in it. So, the main intention is to reduce the number of line by moving condition to filter. 

If the condition is defined in a separate method, it makes the intention explicit and makes the code more readable.

Is it possible to use some other data-structure in the exception to store yang-resource name and checksum. Currently it uses two string, which is not intuitive as there can be multiple yang resources for which it can fail.

there is a lot of code to add correct detail to these exception but this test doesn't verify any of that. It might be good to add bit more expectation. as simple option might be 
somethign like expectedException.toString().contains(expectsExceptionFragment)
don't think this is needed in this case
dnt think this is need following the naming convention (correctly) would make this clear it is a (groovy) Specification for the CpsModulePersistenceService  class

it seems that this test focusses on the error handling only (as positive scenarios are covered by integration tets, which is great!)

so I would suggest to name this "CpsModulePersistenceServiceErrorHandlingSpec"
just check this exception is on the same level as DataValidationException not breaking teh level limit :-)

Why is this a global constant when it's just referenced once in the class?

Could it not just be used as a literal String below?
This is refactored with separated dedicated methods. Seems better now ...
Only one checksum (and its corresponding name) is expected in the exception. This is the unique checksum coming from the db exception.

I don't see this specification test only dedicated to error handling. Additional other unit tests could be added later on depending on coming changes. 
I see this class as being the default specification unit test for `CpsModulePersistenceService` class. Then, simply naming this class `CpsModulePersistenceServiceSpec` would make sense to me.
The only reason why I did not give this name in this change is that this class was already existing and it would have been more difficult to follow the changes made here.
If we agree on this final name https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-493 is created for renaming after this change is reviewed and merged.

Previous `CpsModulePersistenceServiceSpec` is renamed `CpsModulePersistenceServiceIntegrationSpec` to reflect that this one is integration testing.
ok, is fine I did wonder about future additional test...
When not forced by Sonar, I prefer to use explicit types for better clarity and to know what we are manipulating.
If multiple yang resources have checksum issues, does it report only one or all? If it reports more than one, then the existing code will return 'checksumInerror' as null
Can use Optional if null is expected
If checksum or yangresources are null, do we still want to send DuplicatedYangResourceException or the original exception is good.
Should use Optional is null is expected.
can return optional if null is expected.
no need of null check if optional is used in getDuplicatedChecksumFromException method

continuing looping over yangresources even if expected yang resource is found.

Suggestion: can also use stream.filter().findFirst.

As discussed, only one checksum is expected in the db exception. 
If ever, for any unexpected reason, checksumInError is null, it is not a blocker, we still want to throw a DuplicatedYangResourceException, it does not change the process.

We want to throw a DuplicatedYangResourceException as soon as the error is related to YANG_RESOURCE_CHECKSUM_CONSTRAINT_NAME violation, even if checksumInError or nameInError are null. These 2 are optional additional information to provide more context in the exception, but not absolutely required.
no pint for this constant if it is only used to create the constant pattern below (non-blocking)
Do we want to print this error? The similar message is also in the exception. Keeping it for retry scenario

Can get rid of extra null check.

suggestion: YANG_RESOURCE_CHECKSUM_CONSTRAINT_NAME.equals(constraintException.getConstraintName())

maybe the basic null check are better suited for this use-case? otherwise the optional adds lots of extra method calls.

Sorry about the Optional suggestion 😞
